stocks.name: Butterfly Network
stocks.explanation: Shares surged after Midjourney unveiled a full-body ultrasound scanner built with 40 Butterfly ultrasound-on-chip modules. The five-year licensing and co-development agreement could generate up to $74 million in payments for Butterfly. This marks Midjourney's first hardware initiative and validates Butterfly's chip-based imaging platform for AI-driven diagnostics.

stocks.name: Wolfspeed Inc.
stocks.explanation: Wolfspeed rallied on a GE Aerospace MOU to supply 10 kV silicon carbide MOSFET die and co-develop high-voltage SiC modules for AI, aerospace, and defense markets. The company also launched fifth-generation SiC MOSFETs and 3.3 kV power module families targeting AI data centers and grid-scale renewables. With ~33% short interest, positive catalysts triggered significant short-covering.

stocks.name: Accenture plc
stocks.explanation: Accenture slashed its full-year revenue growth forecast to 3-4% from 3-5% after Q3 earnings, warning of a cumulative $400 million hit to EMEA operations from geopolitical disruption. The guidance cut, combined with Q4 revenue projections below Wall Street consensus, triggered the steepest single-day decline in over a year and dragged global IT services peers lower.

stocks.name: EPAM Systems
stocks.explanation: EPAM fell sharply in sympathy with Accenture's weak guidance, which raised concerns about slowing enterprise IT spending across the outsourcing sector. As a major Eastern European-headquartered IT services provider, EPAM faces additional headwinds from geopolitical uncertainty in the region. The selloff reflects broader market fears that AI-driven automation is cannibalizing traditional consulting and outsourcing revenue.

stocks.name: Corning Inc.
stocks.explanation: Corning surged as investors priced in the company's role as a critical physical infrastructure provider for AI data centers. GPU clusters require massive fiber-optic bandwidth, and Corning's recent multiyear, multibillion-dollar supply agreements with major hyperscalers like Amazon have positioned it as an indispensable backbone supplier for the AI buildout.

stocks.name: ZoomInfo Technologies
stocks.explanation: ZoomInfo gained on renewed investor interest in B2B data and sales intelligence platforms as enterprise AI adoption accelerates demand for high-quality business data. The stock has been volatile amid ongoing speculation about strategic alternatives and potential acquisition interest from larger enterprise software players.

stocks.name: Cognizant Technology Solutions
